title: Karnataka government bans hijabs in educational institutions
summary: After ABVP-linked student groups in Udupi blocked hijab-wearing students from entering classrooms, the BJP-governed Karnataka state government issued a circular banning the wearing of clothes that 'disturb equality, integrity and public order' in educational institutions. Muslim women students were excluded from classrooms; protests spread across the state. HRW and Amnesty documented the discrimination and chilling effect on education access.
outcome: The Karnataka High Court upheld the ban in March 2022. The Supreme Court heard an appeal; a two-judge bench gave a split verdict in October 2022. The matter was referred to a larger bench and remained pending as of 2026. The Congress government elected in 2023 signalled review of the circular.

title: Demolitions target Muslim-owned properties after communal incidents
summary: Following communal incidents in Khargone (Madhya Pradesh), Jahangirpuri (Delhi), and Prayagraj and Kanpur (Uttar Pradesh), state and municipal authorities used bulldozers to demolish properties and structures predominantly belonging to Muslim residents and accused persons before any trial or conviction. Critics and courts called the practice collective punishment and extra-judicial demolition. HRW documented the pattern as targeting minorities under the guise of illegal encroachment.
outcome: The Supreme Court intervened to stay demolitions in Jahangirpuri in April 2022. Multiple high courts criticised the demolitions as unconstitutional. The practice continued in BJP-governed states in 2023 and 2024; the Supreme Court issued broader guidelines in October 2024 restricting demolitions without due process.

title: Uttar Pradesh ordinance criminalises interfaith conversions for marriage
summary: Uttar Pradesh promulgated the Prohibition of Unlawful Conversion of Religion Ordinance 2020, criminalising conversions carried out by misrepresentation, force, allurement, or for the purpose of marriage. Rights groups called it a 'love jihad' law targeting Muslim men in interfaith relationships. Madhya Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand, and Gujarat passed similar laws. HRW documented dozens of arrests under UP's ordinance, most of which were against Muslim men.
outcome: Courts in several states stayed or questioned provisions of the laws. The Allahabad High Court ruled that an adult's choice of partner cannot be controlled by the state. As of 2026, prosecutions under the laws continued; most arrests did not result in conviction.

title: Tabrez Ansari lynched and forced to chant Jai Shri Ram, Jharkhand
summary: Tabrez Ansari, 24, was tied to a pole, beaten for hours by a mob in Seraikela-Kharsawan, Jharkhand, and forced to chant Jai Shri Ram and Jai Hanuman. He was handed to police who kept him in custody rather than hospitalising him; he died in custody four days later. The incident was captured on video and spread widely.
outcome: Police initially charged 13 people. A charge of murder was downgraded to culpable homicide after a post-mortem dispute. Multiple accused were granted bail. The Jharkhand government was criticised for the police response. The case was one of several referred to in HRW's 2019 report on cow-vigilantism and Jai Shri Ram violence.

title: Pehlu Khan lynched by cow-vigilante mob, Alwar
summary: Pehlu Khan, a dairy farmer from Haryana, was beaten to death by a mob in Alwar, Rajasthan while transporting cattle he had purchased legally. His sons and companions were also beaten. The attackers were identified by witnesses and survivors. HRW documented the case as part of a broader pattern of cow-vigilante violence enabled by official inaction.
outcome: Police initially filed charges against Pehlu Khan himself and his companions for alleged illegal transport. Charges against six named vigilante accused were dropped after a lower court found insufficient evidence; a higher court later challenged the discharge. No conviction had been secured as of 2026.

title: Junaid Khan killed on train by mob over beef accusations
summary: Sixteen-year-old Junaid Khan was stabbed to death on a train near Ballabhgarh, Haryana, by a mob that targeted him and his brothers with slurs about beef and their Muslim identity. His brothers were also injured. The attack occurred during Eid shopping. The case drew national protests under the #NotInMyName movement.
outcome: Naresh Kumar was convicted of culpable homicide not amounting to murder and sentenced; he appealed. Other accused were acquitted at trial level. Police handling was criticised for initial reluctance to register a communal angle.

title: VHP-RSS Ghar Wapsi mass reconversion drive
summary: The VHP and Dharm Jagran Samanvay Samiti held mass reconversion ceremonies in Agra and Aligarh, enrolling hundreds of Muslim and Christian families into Hinduism. Organisers announced plans to reconvert tens of thousands more and described the drive as reversing historical conversions. Critics cited coercion, false inducements, and the lack of informed consent.
outcome: Parliament was disrupted; opposition demanded a law against forced conversions. The government distanced itself but no organisers were prosecuted. The Ghar Wapsi drive accelerated pressure for anti-conversion laws in BJP-governed states.

title: Vyapam admissions and recruitment scandal escalates
summary: The Madhya Pradesh admissions and recruitment scam drew national scrutiny as witnesses, accused persons, and people connected to the case died in suspicious or unexplained circumstances.
outcome: The Supreme Court transferred the probe to the CBI in 2015 after public pressure over institutional credibility.

title: Badaun deaths and caste-policing failure
summary: Two Dalit teenage cousins were found dead in Badaun, Uttar Pradesh. Early handling by local police became a national example of alleged caste bias and refusal or delay in registering complaints.
outcome: The case remained controversial because the initial public narrative, policing failures, and later investigative findings diverged sharply.

title: Land acquisition ordinance bypasses ordinary passage
summary: The government used ordinance powers to amend land acquisition law after parliamentary contestation, drawing criticism from farmers and opposition parties over consent and social-impact safeguards.
outcome: The ordinance path became an early flashpoint over executive power and rural consent.

title: Delhi Uber rape case exposes vetting failure
summary: A woman passenger was raped by an Uber driver in Delhi. Reporting and police statements pointed to failures in driver background checks and forged police-clearance paperwork.
outcome: The case forced scrutiny of app-based transport regulation and the gap between platform growth and public-safety enforcement.

title: FTII student strike over Gajendra Chauhan appointment
summary: FTII students launched a long strike after actor and BJP member Gajendra Chauhan was appointed chairman, arguing that the appointment reflected political capture of a premier cultural institution.
outcome: The strike lasted roughly 139-140 days and remained a major symbol of ideological appointments in public institutions.

title: Dadri lynching and cow-vigilantism flashpoint
summary: Mohammad Akhlaq was killed by a mob in Dadri after accusations that beef was stored in his home. The case became a national symbol of cow-vigilante violence.
outcome: HRW later placed Dadri within a wider cow-vigilante pattern. The political response around the case sharpened national debate on majoritarian mobilization and impunity.

title: Land acquisition ordinance re-promulgated
summary: After resistance in Parliament, the land acquisition amendment ordinance was re-promulgated, keeping alive changes opposed by farmers and civil-society groups.
outcome: The government withdrew the ordinance in August 2015 after Parliament declined to pass it.

title: M.M. Kalburgi killing and Award Wapsi protests
summary: Rationalist scholar M.M. Kalburgi was shot dead at his home in Dharwad. Reporting described his run-ins with hardliners and the Hindutva fringe; his killing became one trigger for writers returning awards in protest.
outcome: No conviction was secured as of 2026. Karnataka Police linked the killing to Sanatan Sanstha elements. The murder preceded the broader Award Wapsi protest movement.

title: Greenpeace India FCRA suspension
summary: The Ministry of Home Affairs suspended Greenpeace India's foreign-funding license and froze accounts, accusing the group of harming national economic interests.
outcome: The action became an early marker in the decade-long conflict between the state and civil society organizations using FCRA restrictions, later cited by HRW as part of a wider toolkit against activists and NGOs.

title: Supreme Court transfers Vyapam probe to CBI
summary: After national outrage over the Madhya Pradesh Vyapam recruitment and admissions scam and dozens of suspicious deaths linked to people around the case, the Supreme Court ordered a CBI investigation.
outcome: The CBI took over hundreds of cases. Dozens of people connected to the case died under disputed circumstances. Convictions in individual sub-cases followed over subsequent years.

title: Lalitgate travel-document controversy
summary: External Affairs Minister Sushma Swaraj and Rajasthan Chief Minister Vasundhara Raje came under fire over assistance or support connected to Lalit Modi's UK travel documents while he faced Indian investigations.
outcome: The controversy became an early test of the BJP's anti-corruption positioning after 2014.

title: Una Dalit flogging by cow-protection vigilantes
summary: Four Dalit youths were stripped, tied to a vehicle, and flogged in Una, Gujarat, by cow-protection vigilantes on the pretext of skinning a dead cow.
outcome: The attack triggered large Dalit protests and sharpened scrutiny of vigilante violence.

title: JNU sedition clampdown
summary: JNU student leader Kanhaiya Kumar and others faced sedition proceedings after a campus event. HRW called the use of sedition against outspoken activists politically motivated.
outcome: The case became a national marker for anti-national rhetoric, campus policing, and colonial-era sedition law.

title: Rohith Vemula institutional suicide crisis
summary: Dalit scholar Rohith Vemula died by suicide after suspension and institutional pressure at the University of Hyderabad, triggering national protests over caste discrimination in higher education.
outcome: The case remains a core reference point for caste, campus autonomy, and political pressure in universities.

title: Vijay Mallya leaves India amid bank-default scrutiny
summary: Vijay Mallya left India for Britain while banks were seeking repayment of more than $1 billion tied to Kingfisher Airlines, triggering political scrutiny over how he was able to depart despite creditor pressure.
outcome: UK courts upheld the extradition order in 2021 and again in 2022. Mallya's further appeals continued; no extradition had occurred as of 2026.

title: Uttarakhand President's Rule crisis
summary: President's Rule was imposed in Uttarakhand after a rebellion in the Congress-led state government. A Supreme Court-supervised floor test restored Harish Rawat.
outcome: The case belongs in the federalism and Article 356 misuse bucket.

title: Demonetization invalidates most cash overnight
summary: The government invalidated Rs 500 and Rs 1000 notes, removing a large share of circulating cash overnight and severely disrupting cash-dependent households, small businesses, and informal work.
outcome: RBI later reported that almost all demonetized notes returned to the banking system, undercutting the claim that large hidden cash would be extinguished.

title: AgustaWestland chopper scandal returns to Parliament
summary: Italian court developments in the VVIP helicopter bribery matter reignited Indian political confrontation over the AgustaWestland deal signed before 2014.
outcome: The deal was cancelled in 2014. Indian middleman Christian Michel was extradited from the UAE in 2018 and remained on trial.

title: Kashmir unrest and pellet-gun injuries under PDP-BJP government
summary: After Burhan Wani's killing, protests in Jammu and Kashmir were met with pellet guns, tear gas, and live ammunition. HRW called for investigation into lethal force after more than 30 deaths and hundreds of injuries.
outcome: The BJP connection is indirect but real through the central government and the then PDP-BJP state coalition.

title: GST rollout disrupts small businesses and revenue systems
summary: Reuters reported that GST compliance glitches, ambiguous rules, and a burdensome filing system complicated business and hurt collections after the July 2017 launch.
outcome: The government undertook multiple rounds of rate rationalization and simplification. Compliance burden for smaller firms remained a persistent criticism.

title: Election Commission Gujarat poll-date delay row
summary: The Election Commission announced Himachal Pradesh poll dates while delaying Gujarat's schedule, prompting criticism that the ruling party received more time for announcements before the Model Code of Conduct applied.
outcome: Official notices confirm staggered announcement dates; motive remains contested.

title: Anonymous Electoral Bonds route introduced
summary: Legal changes enabled anonymous electoral bonds and weakened disclosure of political funding. Critics, including institutional actors, warned of opacity and corporate influence.
outcome: The Supreme Court struck the scheme down in 2024 as unconstitutional and violative of voters' right to information.

title: RBI data undercuts demonetization black-money claim
summary: RBI reporting showed almost all demonetized notes returned to banks, weakening the claim that the shock would extinguish large amounts of illegal cash.
outcome: This is the clean dated follow-up to the November 2016 shock.

title: Unnao rape case involving BJP MLA Kuldeep Singh Sengar
summary: A minor in Uttar Pradesh accused sitting BJP MLA Kuldeep Singh Sengar of rape. The case drew national outrage after alleged police inaction, intimidation of the family, the custodial death of the victim's father, and a later vehicle crash involving the victim.
outcome: Sengar was expelled from the BJP and sentenced to life imprisonment in December 2019.

title: Gauri Lankesh assassinated
summary: Journalist Gauri Lankesh was shot dead outside her home in Bengaluru. CPJ recorded her as a murdered journalist and noted her criticism of right-wing extremism.
outcome: The case stays in the press-freedom and ideological-violence bucket with event-specific sourcing.

title: Jay Shah Temple Enterprise revenue controversy
summary: Reporting based on company filings said Temple Enterprise, owned by Jay Shah, son of BJP president Amit Shah, saw revenue rise from Rs 50,000 to more than Rs 80 crore in a year after 2014.
outcome: No court proceedings resulted from the reporting. The Wire defended its publication. BJP mounted a criminal defamation suit that was later stayed.

title: Kathua child rape and political defence of accused
summary: An eight-year-old girl from a nomadic Muslim community was abducted, gang-raped, and murdered in Kathua, Jammu and Kashmir. Two BJP ministers attended a rally supporting the accused and later resigned.
outcome: The case became a defining example of sexual violence, communal polarization, and political shielding of accused persons.

title: Rafale procurement controversy and Supreme Court dismissal
summary: Opposition parties alleged corruption and favoritism in the 36-aircraft Rafale deal and offset arrangements. The Supreme Court rejected petitions seeking a probe and said it found no material showing commercial favoritism.
outcome: The Supreme Court declined to order a CBI probe in December 2018 and dismissed review petitions in 2019. Aircraft were delivered between 2020 and 2022.

title: Nirav Modi and Punjab National Bank fraud
summary: Punjab National Bank disclosed a fraud linked to jeweller Nirav Modi and Mehul Choksi. Reuters later reported that internal findings pointed to risk-control and monitoring lapses across many parts of the bank.
outcome: Nirav Modi was arrested in the UK and extradited to India in 2022; trial continued. Multiple PNB officials were also charged.

title: M.J. Akbar resigns after #MeToo allegations
summary: Junior foreign minister M.J. Akbar resigned after multiple women accused him of sexual harassment during his earlier journalism career. Akbar denied the allegations and said he would contest them in court.
outcome: Akbar resigned from his ministerial post. In 2021, a Delhi court dismissed his criminal defamation lawsuit against journalist Priya Ramani, noting she had the right to speak out about her experience.

title: Judge Loya death probe pleas dismissed
summary: Petitioners sought an independent probe into the death of Judge B.H. Loya, who had heard the Sohrabuddin Sheikh encounter case involving Amit Shah before Shah was discharged. The Supreme Court dismissed the pleas and held the death was due to natural causes.
outcome: This belongs in the contested-institutional-record bucket with the Supreme Court outcome visible.

title: Chanda Kochhar ICICI-Videocon loan case
summary: Chanda Kochhar exited ICICI leadership after allegations connected to Videocon loans and alleged quid-pro-quo investments linked to her husband's company. CBI arrested Chanda and Deepak Kochhar in December 2022.
outcome: Chanda Kochhar was arrested by the CBI in December 2022. She was granted bail and the loan fraud trial continued.

title: CBI midnight removal crisis
summary: The government sent the CBI Director and Special Director on leave in a midnight order and sealed offices amid internal conflict inside India's premier investigation agency.
outcome: The crisis raised direct questions about executive control over investigative independence.

title: IL&FS default and NBFC liquidity shock
summary: IL&FS missed debt obligations, triggering stress in debt markets and fears of contagion across India's non-bank financial sector.
outcome: The event exposed hidden leverage, weak governance, and regulatory blind spots in a major infrastructure-finance group.

title: Citizenship Amendment Act passes
summary: Parliament enacted CAA, fast-tracking citizenship for non-Muslim migrants from Pakistan, Afghanistan, and Bangladesh. Critics said it made religion a basis for citizenship and threatened constitutional secularism.
outcome: The law triggered nationwide protests and a severe police response in several BJP-governed states.

title: Pulwama terror attack
summary: A suicide bomber attacked a CRPF convoy in Pulwama, killing dozens of Indian security personnel. Jaish-e-Mohammad claimed responsibility, and the attack led to a sharp India-Pakistan escalation.
outcome: India conducted air strikes at Balakot, Pakistan on 26 February 2019. The escalation immediately preceded the 2019 general election.

title: Kuldeep Singh Sengar sentenced to life
summary: A Delhi court sentenced expelled BJP MLA Kuldeep Singh Sengar to life imprisonment for the rape of a minor in the Unnao case.
outcome: The conviction followed years of alleged intimidation and delayed accountability.

title: Swami Chinmayanand sexual-assault case
summary: Former Union minister and BJP leader Swami Chinmayanand was arrested after a law student accused him of sexual assault and exploitation. BJP later claimed he was no longer a party member.
outcome: A court convicted Chinmayanand of harassment in 2023. The law student was also convicted of extortion. Both sides appealed.

title: Article 370 revoked and Jammu and Kashmir downgraded
summary: The government revoked Jammu and Kashmir's special status, split the state into union territories, imposed a heavy security lockdown, restricted movement, and cut communications. HRW documented the communications blackout and preventive detentions that followed the 5 August 2019 decision.
outcome: The decision reshaped federalism and civil-liberties debate in India. Communications restrictions and detentions became a separate rights record alongside the constitutional change.

title: Deadly crackdown on anti-CAA protests
summary: Human Rights Watch reported at least 25 people killed after protests against CAA began, with deaths in BJP-governed Uttar Pradesh, Assam, and Karnataka.
outcome: The crackdown became a major evidence point for excessive force, internet shutdowns, and criminalization of dissent around the Citizenship Amendment Act protests.

title: Hathras assault, death, and forced cremation
summary: A 19-year-old Dalit woman in Hathras died after an assault. Police cremated her body late at night while the family was kept away, creating national outrage over caste, gender violence, and state handling.
outcome: The case should be categorized under sexual violence and state-institution failure, with careful wording because court findings did not accept every initial public claim.

title: Delhi communal violence and biased investigation allegations
summary: Delhi's worst communal violence in decades left 53 people dead and hundreds injured. HRW cited findings that police were complicit or failed to act while BJP leaders used inflammatory rhetoric against anti-CAA protesters.
outcome: More than 1,900 FIRs were filed. Critics documented that UAPA and related charges were disproportionately applied to activists and journalists. HRW later noted that Umar Khalid, Sharjeel Imam, Gulfisha Fatima, and others remained jailed for years without trial in related cases.

title: Four-hour-notice national Covid lockdown
summary: India announced a nationwide Covid lockdown with only hours of notice. The sudden stoppage stranded migrant workers without wages, transport, food security, or housing protections.
outcome: The migration crisis became one of the largest visible humanitarian failures of the first Covid year.

title: Farmers' march to Delhi met with tear gas and water cannon
summary: Farmers marching toward Delhi against the three farm laws faced barricades, tear gas, and water cannon. The protest became a year-long mass mobilization at the capital's borders.
outcome: The government repealed the farm laws in November 2021 after sustained pressure.

title: Three farm laws pass and trigger mass mobilization
summary: Parliament passed three farm laws in September 2020. PRS records that the 2021 repeal bill repealed those three Acts; protesters argued the original process and market changes threatened farmer protections.
outcome: After over a year of protests and more than 700 farmer deaths documented by protest organisations, Parliament repealed all three laws in November 2021.

title: Pegasus surveillance disclosures
summary: The Pegasus Project reported that Indian numbers connected to journalists, human-rights defenders, lawyers, officials, and opposition politicians appeared in leaked potential-target lists. Amnesty's forensic work confirmed infections in selected cases.
outcome: The government denied unauthorized surveillance. A Supreme Court-appointed technical committee found no conclusive evidence of Pegasus malware on the 29 devices it inspected, but noted that the government did not cooperate fully with its inquiry.

title: Farm laws repealed after year-long protest
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced the decision to repeal the three farm laws on 19 November 2021. PRS records the Farm Laws Repeal Bill being passed by both houses on 29 November 2021.
outcome: The repeal shows mass protest forcing policy reversal; it should be recorded as an official rollback, not merely a protest claim.

title: Elgar Parishad / Bhima Koregaon UAPA imprisonments
summary: Activists, lawyers, academics, and a priest were arrested under UAPA in the Bhima Koregaon case. Father Stan Swamy died in custody in 2021. Later court orders and forensic reporting raised serious doubts about parts of the evidence.
outcome: This should be tracked as one of the decade's central anti-terror-law and political-prisoner controversies.

title: Assam Darrang eviction firing and Moinul Haque killing
summary: During a state eviction drive in Darrang, Assam police opened fire on residents; Moinul Haque and a 12-year-old, Sheikh Farid, died. Video of a government photographer attacking Haque's body went viral.
outcome: The event is directly relevant to BJP-governed Assam and belongs in police-brutality and minority-displacement tracking.

title: Lakhimpur Kheri killings during farmers' protest
summary: During farm-law protests in Lakhimpur Kheri, four farmers and a journalist were killed after vehicles ran over protesters. The UP Police SIT later chargesheeted Union minister Ajay Mishra's son Ashish Mishra and others under murder and conspiracy provisions.
outcome: Ashish Mishra was arrested after Supreme Court direction. He was granted bail and re-arrested. Trial proceedings continued as of 2024.

title: Covid second wave oxygen and hospital collapse
summary: India's Delta-driven second wave overwhelmed hospitals and oxygen supply. HRW reported shortages in oxygen, medicines, beds, ambulances, and cremation or burial services.
outcome: WHO excess-mortality modelling later estimated India had one of the world's largest excess-death burdens for 2020-2021, though the Indian government disputed such estimates.

title: Bilkis Bano convicts released by Gujarat remission order
summary: The BJP-led Gujarat government released 11 men serving life sentences for gang-raping Bilkis Bano and murdering members of her family during the 2002 riots.
outcome: In January 2024, the Supreme Court quashed the remission and said the Gujarat government lacked jurisdiction.

title: Ankita Bhandari murder and VIP coercion allegations
summary: Ankita Bhandari, a 19-year-old resort receptionist in Uttarakhand, was killed after resisting pressure to provide special services to guests. The main accused was Pulkit Arya, son of former BJP leader Vinod Arya.
outcome: In 2025, Pulkit Arya and two others were sentenced to life imprisonment.

title: Kanhaiya Lal murder after post supporting Nupur Sharma
summary: Tailor Kanhaiya Lal Teli was murdered in Udaipur. Reuters reported that the accused said the killing was in response to the victim's support for Nupur Sharma's remarks about Prophet Mohammed.
outcome: This is not a BJP culpability event; it is included because it is directly connected to the BJP remarks controversy and violent communal escalation.

title: Morbi suspension bridge collapse
summary: A suspension bridge in Morbi, Gujarat collapsed days after reopening, killing 135 people. Local reporting found it had reopened without a fitness certificate and without proper structural testing.
outcome: A Gujarat SIT later cited corroded wires and welded suspenders among major faults behind the disaster.

title: BJP spokesperson Prophet remarks trigger diplomatic backlash
summary: BJP spokesperson Nupur Sharma was suspended and Naveen Jindal expelled after remarks about Prophet Mohammed triggered condemnation from Muslim-majority countries and police complaints in Delhi.
outcome: The event is a direct BJP communication and communal-friction controversy; subsequent violence should be separately sourced.

title: Silkyara tunnel collapse traps 41 workers for 17 days
summary: A Himalayan highway tunnel under construction in Uttarakhand collapsed, trapping 41 workers for 17 days before all were rescued.
outcome: The collapse triggered safety audits of other tunnels and raised questions about risk controls in fragile Himalayan infrastructure projects.

title: BBC India offices raided after Modi documentary
summary: Tax authorities searched BBC offices in Delhi and Mumbai weeks after the broadcaster aired a documentary critical of Narendra Modi's handling of the 2002 Gujarat violence.
outcome: HRW described the searches as an apparent reprisal and part of pressure on free speech.

title: Balasore three-train collision
summary: A three-train collision in Odisha killed at least 288 people and injured more than 800. Reuters reported that a preliminary report blamed signal failure.
outcome: The disaster forced scrutiny of railway safety systems, signaling, and modernization claims.

title: IIT-BHU gang-rape case and BJP IT cell arrests
summary: A 20-year-old IIT-BHU student was allegedly gang-raped on campus in November 2023. Times of India reported that two of three men arrested later were BJP IT cell office-bearers in Varanasi.
outcome: Two accused were arrested; trial commenced. The university administration was criticised for its initial response.

title: Adani-Hindenburg crisis
summary: Hindenburg Research accused the Adani Group of improper use of offshore tax havens and stock manipulation; the Adani Group denied wrongdoing. Reuters reported the report triggered a major listed-stock rout and regulator scrutiny.
outcome: The Adani Group denied all allegations of manipulation and fraud. The Supreme Court directed SEBI to complete its investigation, and SEBI subsequently conducted inquiries into the group's transactions.

title: Rahul Gandhi disqualified, then reinstated after Supreme Court stay
summary: Rahul Gandhi was disqualified from the Lok Sabha after a Gujarat court convicted him in a defamation case brought by a BJP lawmaker over a Modi-surname remark. Parliament reinstated him after the Supreme Court stayed the conviction.
outcome: Freedom House cited the episode under concerns about political-opposition space and institutions.

title: Manipur ethnic violence begins
summary: Violence erupted between Meitei and Kuki-Zo communities in Manipur. HRW reported allegations of police bias and divisive state policies under the BJP-led state government.
outcome: By 2025, rights groups reported more than 260 people killed and over 60,000 displaced.

title: NewsClick UAPA raids and arrests
summary: Delhi Police raided homes and offices linked to NewsClick and arrested founder Prabir Purkayastha and HR head Amit Chakravarty under UAPA.
outcome: Amnesty called it an attack on media critical of the government and a case of anti-terror law being weaponized against journalism.

title: Women wrestlers protest Brij Bhushan Sharan Singh
summary: Top Indian women wrestlers protested demanding action against BJP MP and wrestling federation chief Brij Bhushan Sharan Singh over sexual harassment allegations.
outcome: A Delhi court later ordered framing of sexual harassment and criminal intimidation charges. Singh denied wrongdoing.

title: Nuh violence and demolition aftermath
summary: Communal violence broke out in Haryana's Nuh district after a religious procession. Authorities later carried out demolition drives that critics described as punitive and discriminatory.
outcome: The episode became part of the broader national record on vigilante mobilization and administrative punishment, including demolition drives criticized as punitive.

title: SEBI chief conflict-of-interest allegations in Adani matter
summary: Hindenburg alleged that SEBI chair Madhabi Puri Buch and her husband had held investments in offshore funds also used by Adani-linked entities. Buch, SEBI, Adani-linked entities, and 360 ONE denied or disputed key allegations.
outcome: Buch and SEBI issued statements denying the conflict-of-interest allegations, terming them baseless, while opposition parties called for an independent inquiry.

title: One Nation, One Election federalism controversy
summary: The Kovind committee submitted an 18,626-page report backing simultaneous elections. Opposition parties told the panel the proposal was anti-federal and against the basic structure, while the committee rejected those objections.
outcome: The Constitution (One Hundred and Twenty-Ninth Amendment) Bill, 2024 was introduced in December 2024 and referred to a Joint Parliamentary Committee. It had not passed as of mid-2026.

title: Supreme Court strikes down Electoral Bonds
summary: The Supreme Court held the Electoral Bonds scheme unconstitutional, finding that anonymous political funding violated voters' right to information.
outcome: ECI published SBI bond purchase and redemption data, enabling public scrutiny of party funding patterns.

title: Enforcement Directorate opposition-targeting pattern
summary: Indian Express reported a four-fold jump in ED cases against politicians after 2014, with 115 of 121 prominent politicians under ED scanner through September 2022 being opposition leaders.
outcome: Official ED statistics separately show large PMLA activity through 31 March 2026; the political-targeting claim should be presented as investigative analysis, not official ED admission.

title: Press freedom rank reaches 159/180
summary: RSF ranked India 159 out of 180 in the 2024 World Press Freedom Index, reflecting concerns over violence against journalists, media ownership concentration, and political pressure.
outcome: The 2024 rank of 159 sits between the 2014 rank of 140 and later ranks of 151 (2025) and 157 (2026) in the site's RSF indicator series. Methodology changes mean year-to-year ranks should be read carefully.

title: ADR flags lawmakers with crimes-against-women cases
summary: ADR reported that 151 sitting MPs and MLAs had declared cases related to crimes against women. BJP had the highest party count at 54, followed by Congress at 23 and TDP at 17.
outcome: ADR also found 16 sitting lawmakers had declared rape cases, with BJP and Congress at five each.

title: Supreme Court reverses Bilkis Bano remission
summary: The Supreme Court quashed remission granted to the 11 Bilkis Bano convicts, holding that Gujarat did not have jurisdiction to decide their release.
outcome: The judgment turned the 2022 remission into a concrete court-tested governance failure.

title: RG Kar doctor rape and murder
summary: The rape and murder of a postgraduate trainee doctor inside Kolkata's RG Kar hospital triggered nationwide medical protests over workplace safety, institutional accountability, and evidence handling.
outcome: HRW's 2026 India chapter cited continuing failures in protecting women and girls and barriers to justice.

title: Prajwal Revanna sexual-abuse scandal
summary: Karnataka MP Prajwal Revanna of JD(S), then a BJP ally, faced multiple sexual-abuse allegations after videos surfaced during the 2024 election period. He denied wrongdoing in public legal proceedings.
outcome: A special court later convicted and sentenced him to life imprisonment in August 2025 in one rape case; other case status should be tracked separately.

title: NEET-UG 2024 irregularities and admitted leak
summary: The Supreme Court observed that a NEET-UG 2024 leak had taken place while considering petitions from candidates. More than 23 lakh students were affected by uncertainty over the exam.
outcome: The court did not order a full retest, saying evidence did not show a systemic breach of the entire exam.

title: Prajwal Revanna convicted and sentenced to life imprisonment
summary: A special court convicted former JD(S) MP Prajwal Revanna on 1 August 2025 and sentenced him to life imprisonment the next day in the rape of a domestic worker. He had been the JD(S)-BJP alliance candidate in Hassan in 2024.
outcome: This updates the 2024 scandal with a court-tested conviction in one of the registered cases.

title: Mukesh Chandrakar murder after corruption reporting
summary: Journalist Mukesh Chandrakar, who reported on corruption and local governance in Chhattisgarh, was found killed in a septic tank on a contractor's property. CPJ recorded the case and said police alleged the killing was retaliation for reporting on road construction.
outcome: Contractor Suresh Chandrakar was named the primary suspect and was arrested in January 2025. Trial proceedings began.

title: Delhi Red Fort car explosion investigated under terror law
summary: A car explosion near Delhi's Red Fort killed at least 13 people and wounded more than 20. Police invoked UAPA, and Al Jazeera later reported an NIA arrest of a Kashmir resident as an alleged accomplice.
outcome: One arrest was made under UAPA. The NIA took over the investigation. Attribution remained contested as of 2026.

title: Air India Flight 171 crash
summary: Air India Flight AI-171, a Boeing 787-8 from Ahmedabad to London, crashed shortly after takeoff on 12 June 2025, killing 260 people according to the official accident-investigation statement.
outcome: All 241 people on board died, making it the deadliest aviation accident on Indian soil in decades. The AAIB investigation was ongoing and an aviation-safety review was ordered.

title: President's Rule imposed in Manipur
summary: President's Rule was imposed in Manipur after renewed ethnic clashes and the resignation of Chief Minister N. Biren Singh.
outcome: HRW said the state and central governments should use the period to end abuses, expand aid, and rebuild trust.

title: VB-G RAM G replaces MGNREGA framework
summary: The Viksit Bharat Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission (Gramin) Bill, 2025 replaced MGNREGA while increasing the statutory employment guarantee from 100 to 125 days.
outcome: The bill passed in November 2025. Labour unions and opposition parties raised concerns about the replacement of MGNREGA entitlements and new conditionality provisions.

title: 130th Constitutional Amendment Bill on detained ministers
summary: Amit Shah introduced the Constitution (130th Amendment) Bill, 2025, proposing removal of prime ministers, chief ministers, and ministers detained for 30 consecutive days in serious-offence cases.
outcome: PRS flagged basic-structure concerns around automatic removal; the government framed it as anti-corruption reform.

title: SSC exam glitches and aspirant protests
summary: Staff Selection Commission exams faced cancellations, software crashes, biometric failures, and wrong centre allotments. Indian Express reported thousands of aspirants protested and the SSC chairman later said technical and operational issues were being resolved.
outcome: This is separate from NEET but belongs in the exam-integrity and youth-accountability track.

title: Leh statehood protests, four killed, Sonam Wangchuk held under NSA
summary: After protests in Leh over statehood and protections for local land and jobs turned violent on 24 September 2025, four people were killed and many injured. Reuters reporting said police arrested activist Sonam Wangchuk on 26-27 September under the National Security Act; authorities alleged he had incited violence, while his movement had publicly used hunger strikes and marches.
outcome: Mobile internet was suspended in Leh and Wangchuk was transferred to Jodhpur detention under the National Security Act. The record separates the authorities' incitement allegation from the unresolved question of accountability for the protest violence; no statehood settlement followed.

title: Kerala nuns arrested in Chhattisgarh after Bajrang Dal complaint
summary: Two Kerala nuns were arrested at Durg railway station in Chhattisgarh on allegations of forced conversion and human trafficking. Indian Express later reported the state women's commission asked police to register an FIR against right-wing workers accused of harassment in the episode.
outcome: The nuns were released. The women's commission demanded FIRs against Bajrang Dal workers for harassment during the arrest. The conversion case continued.

title: Sanchar Saathi preinstall order revoked after backlash
summary: The Department of Telecommunications ordered smartphone makers to preinstall the state-owned Sanchar Saathi app on new devices, then revoked the mandate after backlash over privacy, security, and user autonomy.
outcome: The mandatory preinstallation requirement was withdrawn after industry and rights-group pushback. The app moved to an opt-in model.

title: Jammu and Kashmir VPN ban
summary: District authorities in Kashmir ordered a two-month VPN ban citing national-security risks. Al Jazeera reported residents and professionals saying the ban added pressure and disrupted secure work.
outcome: This belongs with the Kashmir communications-restriction record and should be updated if the order lapses, is extended, or is challenged.

title: Waqf Amendment Act protests and Murshidabad violence
summary: After Parliament cleared the Waqf Amendment framework, protests in West Bengal's Murshidabad district turned violent. NDTV reported three deaths and more than 100 arrests.
outcome: Three people were killed. Paramilitary forces were deployed to Murshidabad. Internet shutdowns were imposed. Responsibility for the violence remained contested.

title: Reuters X accounts withheld in India
summary: Reuters' main X accounts became inaccessible in India with a legal-demand notice; the government said no current legal requirement existed and that it was working with X to resolve the issue.
outcome: This is a concrete example of opaque platform blocking and media-access disruption.

title: Pahalgam tourist terror attack and India-Pakistan escalation
summary: Gunmen killed 26 people, mostly Hindu tourists, in Pahalgam, Jammu and Kashmir. India blamed Pakistan-based groups and launched targeted airstrikes; Pakistan retaliated.
outcome: HRW described the episode as bringing India and Pakistan perilously close to full-blown war.

title: Expulsions of Bengali-speaking Muslims and Rohingya
summary: HRW reported that authorities expelled hundreds of Bengali-speaking Muslims and Rohingya refugees to Bangladesh without due process, including some Indian citizens. The same chapter recorded Assam demolition drives in July–August that displaced more than 5,000 families, majority Bengali-speaking Muslims, and a fatal shooting in Goalpara during eviction clashes.
outcome: Bangladesh authorities reported more than 1,500 men, women, and children expelled in May and June. Assam demolitions and Goalpara killing sit in the same HRW 2026 rights record as the cross-border expulsions.

title: X says India ordered over 8,000 account blocks
summary: During India-Pakistan tensions after the Pahalgam attack, HRW reported that Indian authorities ordered X to block more than 8,000 accounts, with X saying evidence or justification was not provided for most.
outcome: India blocked more than 8,000 accounts on X/Twitter, including Reuters' main account, during the India-Pakistan tensions following the Pahalgam attack.

title: NHRC custody and extrajudicial-killing figures
summary: HRW cited NHRC registrations of 113 deaths in police custody, 1,535 deaths in judicial custody, and 132 alleged extrajudicial killings in the first eight months of 2025.
outcome: HRW reported the NHRC registrations as part of its 2026 India rights assessment; no separate nationwide conviction tally is attached to these counts in the cited source.

title: Adani-related media takedown orders
summary: HRW reported that the Ministry of Information and Broadcasting ordered news organizations, journalists, and creators to take down 138 YouTube videos and 83 Instagram posts allegedly defaming Adani Enterprises.
outcome: The order was catalogued as part of wider pressure on media freedom and online speech.

title: India ranks 157/180 in RSF Press Freedom Index
summary: RSF's India country page lists India at 157 out of 180 in the 2026 World Press Freedom Index, after 151 in 2025 and 140 in 2014.
outcome: India's RSF score fell from 59.66 in 2014 to 31.96 in 2026, placing it in the 'Difficult' category, 17 places lower than at the start of the period.

title: Opposition claim: 44 BJP MPs and MLAs face serious sexual-offence charges
summary: The Indian National Congress claimed that 44 BJP MPs and MLAs were facing serious rape or sexual-harassment charges. This is stored as an opposition claim pending direct ADR or court-record cross-check.
outcome: The stronger current baseline is ADR's 2024 affidavit analysis: BJP had 54 sitting lawmakers with declared cases related to crimes against women and five with declared rape cases.

title: Amnesty warns of renewed hostage crisis in Manipur
summary: Amnesty International warned that hostage-taking by armed groups in Manipur reflected a deepening human-rights crisis and continuing government failure to protect people.
outcome: The report said at least 200 people had been killed and more than 60,000 displaced since 3 May 2023.

title: NEET-UG 2026 paper leak and re-examination
summary: PIB said the CBI arrested a Latur coaching institute owner in the NEET-UG 2026 leak case and recovered a question bank with questions matching the 3 May exam. Indian Express later reported CBI told a Delhi court that 111 of 136 recovered questions matched NTA master sets.
outcome: NTA notices show a NEET-UG 2026 re-examination process in June 2026. The case is a live institutional-integrity failure.

title: Rupee breaches 96 per US dollar and inflation pressure returns
summary: Reuters-linked market reporting said the rupee hit record lows past 96 per US dollar in May 2026 amid oil shocks, yields, and capital-flow pressure. Reuters also reported June 2026 retail inflation at 4.38 percent, above the RBI's 4 percent target for the first time in 17 months.
outcome: The rupee breached 96 per US dollar in May 2026, its lowest-ever level. Retail inflation was 4.38 per cent in June 2026. The RBI maintained rates.

title: BJP wins the 2014 Lok Sabha election; Narendra Modi is chosen prime minister
summary: The BJP won 282 of 543 Lok Sabha seats in the 2014 general election, giving the party a single-party majority. Narendra Modi was appointed prime minister and took office on 26 May 2014.
outcome: The result began the Modi government period documented in this archive. The election result itself is recorded separately from later policy outcomes and controversies.

title: BJP wins a second consecutive Lok Sabha majority
summary: The BJP won 303 Lok Sabha seats in the 2019 general election, increasing its seat total from 282 in 2014. Narendra Modi returned as prime minister for a second consecutive term.
outcome: The result gave the government another single-party majority and preceded the second-term decisions recorded elsewhere in this timeline.

title: BJP remains the largest party as the NDA returns Modi for a third term
summary: The 2024 Lok Sabha election made the BJP the largest party, while its NDA alliance secured the parliamentary majority needed to form the government. Narendra Modi was sworn in for a third term on 9 June 2024.
outcome: The third Modi government is coalition-supported rather than a BJP single-party majority. That distinction is kept visible in the archive.

title: Jammu and Kashmir communications blackout after Article 370
summary: After the Article 370 decision, authorities shut down internet, mobile networks, and for a period landlines across much of Jammu and Kashmir, alongside movement restrictions and preventive detentions. HRW described the blackout as disproportionate and harmful to medical care, livelihoods, and information access.
outcome: HRW's later shutdown report stated the August 2019 internet shutdown lasted 213 days into March 2020, with mobile 4G access restricted for 550 days. The episode remains one of the longest communications blackouts documented in India.

title: Shaheen Bagh sit-in against CAA and NRC fears
summary: A prolonged women-led sit-in at Shaheen Bagh in Delhi became the most visible sustained protest against the Citizenship Amendment Act and linked fears of a nationwide NRC. HRW's CAA reporting documented the discriminatory structure of the law and the repression that surrounded the protest wave.
outcome: The sit-in ended amid the COVID-19 lockdown. It remains a public-record marker of mass constitutional protest and of the policing and political backlash that followed anti-CAA mobilization.

title: Supreme Court Ayodhya title verdict awards disputed site for temple
summary: A five-judge Supreme Court bench delivered the Ayodhya title judgment, awarding the disputed site for a Ram temple while directing alternate land for a mosque. The judgment closed the long-running title litigation after the 1992 Babri Masjid demolition.
outcome: The verdict was a court order, not a parliamentary statute. It remained politically central to Hindutva mobilization and to later consecration politics, while critics treated the communal aftermath as part of the majoritarian public record.

title: Muslim Women (Protection of Rights on Marriage) Act criminalises instant triple talaq
summary: Parliament enacted the Muslim Women (Protection of Rights on Marriage) Act, 2019, declaring instant triple talaq void and illegal and making pronouncement a cognizable offence punishable by up to three years' imprisonment, with subsistence and custody provisions for affected women.
outcome: The government presented the Act as women's-rights reform after Supreme Court scrutiny of talaq-e-biddat. Critics argued criminalisation of a personal-law practice raised due-process and minority-rights concerns even while opposing instant triple talaq itself.

title: Galwan Valley clash kills 20 Indian soldiers on LAC with China
summary: Indian Army statements confirmed 20 Indian soldiers killed in a violent face-off with Chinese troops in the Galwan Valley during a supposed de-escalation along the Line of Actual Control. Reporting described hand-to-hand fighting without firearms.
outcome: The clash was the deadliest India-China border confrontation in decades and reshaped domestic security politics, military posture in Ladakh, and later commemorative politics. Chinese casualty figures were disclosed much later and remain contested in Indian assessments.

title: HRW: at least 44 killed in cow-vigilante attacks
summary: Human Rights Watch reported that between May 2015 and December 2018 at least 44 people were killed in cow-protection vigilante attacks across multiple states, including 36 Muslims, with around 280 people injured in over 100 incidents. The report documented police stalling, victim-blaming, and political rhetoric that accompanied the violence.
outcome: The tally aggregates cases also represented individually on this timeline (Dadri, Pehlu Khan, Junaid Khan, and related attacks). It remains the clearest rights-group quantitative marker for the cow-vigilante wave after 2014.

title: Delhi riots accused remain jailed for years without trial
summary: Umar Khalid and other accused were arrested under the UAPA in 2020 in connection with the Delhi violence and remained in pre-trial detention for years. The Delhi High Court denied regular bail on 2 September 2025; the Supreme Court denied bail to Khalid and Sharjeel Imam on 5 January 2026. The Delhi High Court later granted Khalid three-day interim bail for his mother's surgery in June 2026. The allegations are not convictions.
outcome: The criminal case remained pending without a trial verdict. The record distinguishes arrest, prolonged pre-trial detention, regular-bail decisions, and temporary interim bail from any finding of guilt.

title: HRW documents internet shutdowns as rights deprivation in Digital India
summary: HRW's 2023 report documented how central and state authorities used internet and communications shutdowns to deny access to work, pay, food, education, and emergency information, with Kashmir's post-2019 blackout as the extreme case and India repeatedly ranking among the world's heaviest users of shutdowns.
outcome: The report complements SFLC tracker counts in the statistics section. It treats shutdowns as a governance tool with measurable human costs, not only as temporary security measures.

title: Bihar electoral-roll revision triggers disenfranchisement and institutional-integrity dispute
summary: The Election Commission conducted a Special Intensive Revision of Bihar's electoral rolls before the state election. The Commission said the exercise was intended to remove dead, shifted, absent, or duplicate entries and protect electoral-roll integrity. The BJP-backed exercise drew opposition protests and concerns that documentation requirements could exclude eligible voters; the draft process reported about 65 lakh names not included.
outcome: The Supreme Court directed publication of the omitted names and reasons and allowed Aadhaar and other listed documents for claims. The ECI later published a final roll and said the revision was completed. Allegations that the exercise was designed to benefit the BJP remain contested rather than judicially established.

title: Cabinet approves caste enumeration in the upcoming national census
summary: The Cabinet Committee on Political Affairs approved the inclusion of caste enumeration in the upcoming national census. The government presented the decision as a social-justice and inclusion measure; independent reporting also noted its political significance ahead of Bihar's election and the long-standing opposition demand for updated caste data.
outcome: The decision was incorporated into planning for Census 2027. Detailed implementation, categories, publication, and use of the resulting data remained future questions at the time of the announcement.

title: Balasore investigation and railway signalling accountability
summary: The Coromandel Express, a stationary goods train, and the SMVP–Howrah Superfast Express were involved in a collision and derailment in Odisha. More than 280 people were killed and about 900 injured. Early reporting and the subsequent safety debate focused on signalling and interlocking failures, maintenance, staffing, and the rollout of automatic train-protection systems.
outcome: The Railway Board referred the case for investigation and arrests followed in connection with alleged signalling lapses. The event became a major test of the government's railway modernisation and safety claims; responsibility should be read through the investigation record rather than assigned solely from the political office held at the time.

title: Vizianagaram passenger-train collision kills 13
summary: A passenger train struck a stationary passenger train in Andhra Pradesh's Vizianagaram district. At least 13 people died and 25 were injured. Railway officials cited a preliminary finding of human error, while reporting also placed the accident in the wider context of signalling and operating-safety risks.
outcome: The preliminary attribution was not treated as a final systemic finding in this record. It is included to track operational failures and the accountability question separately from the larger Balasore disaster.

title: Kanchanjunga Express collision kills railway staff and passengers
summary: A cargo train hit the Kanchanjunga Express in West Bengal's Darjeeling district. At least nine people died and dozens were injured. Railway officials said the cargo-train driver had disregarded a signal; the crash renewed questions about automatic protection, staffing, and safety on busy mixed-traffic routes.
outcome: The incident remained subject to investigation. Government safety statistics show a long-run fall in consequential accidents, but individual crashes continue to expose gaps between national safety programmes and conditions on specific routes.

title: Indore-Patna Express derailment near Pukhrayan
summary: The Indore-Patna Express derailed near Pukhrayan in Uttar Pradesh, killing more than 100 people and injuring more than 150. The disaster became one of the deadliest railway accidents during the period and prompted scrutiny of track condition, maintenance, and passenger safety.
outcome: Initial casualty figures changed during rescue operations and reporting. The event is retained with a range rather than a single unsupported final number.

title: Khatauli Utkal Express derailment
summary: The Kalinga Utkal Express derailed near Khatauli in Uttar Pradesh, killing 23 people and injuring more than 150. Reports raised questions about track work, railway maintenance, and whether safety procedures were followed before trains passed through the section.
outcome: The Railway Board ordered an inquiry and disciplinary action was reported. The case is recorded as an infrastructure and operational-safety failure, not as proof of a single political cause.

title: Jalgaon passenger deaths after a fire scare
summary: Passengers jumped from the Pushpak Express after a reported fire scare and were struck by another train on the adjacent track near Jalgaon, Maharashtra. At least 12 people died and several were injured.
outcome: The initial account cited sparks from a possible hot axle or brake binding and passenger panic. The incident highlights emergency communication, platform/track separation, and crowd-safety risks in addition to rolling-stock safety.

title: Bengaluru-Kamakhya Express derailment near Cuttack
summary: Eleven coaches of the Bengaluru-Kamakhya Express derailed near Nergundi in Odisha's Cuttack district. One person died and several passengers were injured. Railway authorities began an inquiry while rescue and route restoration operations continued.
outcome: The cause remained under investigation in the initial reporting. The record therefore separates the confirmed casualties from later claims about track defects or maintenance responsibility.

title: Passenger train collides with cargo train near Bilaspur
summary: A passenger train collided with a cargo train near Bilaspur in central India. At least 11 people died and about 20 were injured. One passenger coach came to rest on a cargo wagon during the crash.
outcome: Indian Railways opened an inquiry and announced compensation. The cause was not treated as established in the initial reports.

title: Vasco Da Gama–Patna Express derailment near Manikpur
summary: Fourteen coaches of the Vasco Da Gama–Patna Express derailed near Manikpur in Uttar Pradesh. The railway ministry's incident release recorded the location, time, train number, and coach derailment details; contemporary reporting recorded deaths and injuries.
outcome: The entry preserves the official incident facts without treating an initial cause or casualty figure as final. It is part of the wider 2017 cluster of railway safety failures.

title: Fire in a parked private train coach at Madurai
summary: A fire inside a detached private coach parked at Madurai station killed nine people and injured others. Southern Railway said a gas cylinder brought into the coach caused the blaze, which burned for about two hours.
outcome: The incident highlights fire prevention, passenger screening, emergency egress, and the separate safety risks of private coaches parked on railway premises.

title: Howrah-Mumbai Mail derailment in Jharkhand
summary: The Howrah-Mumbai Mail derailed in Jharkhand after a reported interaction with a partially derailed goods train on a parallel track. Two people died and 16 were injured in the initial reporting.
outcome: The Commissioner of Railway Safety was tasked with investigating the cause. The record distinguishes the confirmed casualties from preliminary accounts about the sequence of derailment and collision.

title: Rajdhani Express collision kills seven elephants in Assam
summary: A Rajdhani Express struck a herd of elephants in Assam. Seven wild elephants were killed, a calf was injured, and five coaches and the engine derailed; no human deaths were reported in the initial account.
outcome: The collision renewed scrutiny of speed management, wildlife corridors, early-warning systems, and coordination between railway and forest authorities. The site was reported not to be a designated elephant corridor.

title: Bagmati Express collides with a stationary goods train at Kavaraipettai
summary: The Mysuru-Darbhanga Bagmati Express entered a loop line at Kavaraipettai in Tamil Nadu and collided with a stationary goods train. Twelve coaches derailed, a fire broke out in a parcel coach, and early reports recorded injuries but no deaths.
outcome: Railway authorities ordered a high-level inquiry. Officials described a mismatch between the signal and route as a possible factor; the record preserves that as an investigative finding rather than a final judicial conclusion.

title: Chandigarh-Dibrugarh Express derailment near Gonda
summary: Eight coaches of the Chandigarh-Dibrugarh Express derailed between Jhilahi and Motiganj in Uttar Pradesh's Gonda district. Four passengers were later reported dead and 32 injured in parliamentary and press accounts.
outcome: The Railway Ministry ordered a high-level inquiry alongside a Commission of Railway Safety inquiry. Later reporting discussed possible track or equipment failure, but the record does not treat early hypotheses as a final finding.

title: Crowd crush at New Delhi Railway Station during Maha Kumbh travel
summary: A crowd crush at New Delhi Railway Station killed 18 people and injured 15 others as passengers gathered to travel toward the Maha Kumbh in Prayagraj. Early accounts described confusion around platforms and movement near a footbridge.
outcome: The government constituted a high-level inquiry committee. Railway authorities later rejected reports that a separate RPF inquiry had already established a definitive cause, so this record keeps the cause and accountability questions open.

title: Maha Kumbh crowd crush in Prayagraj
summary: A crowd crush during the Maha Kumbh festival in Prayagraj killed at least 30 people and injured about 60, according to police and Associated Press reporting. The crush occurred during a major bathing day when exceptionally large crowds gathered at the festival site.
outcome: Authorities announced an inquiry into the incident. Contemporaneous reporting contained differing unofficial estimates, so the record uses the official police figure available in the cited reporting and does not present higher unofficial counts as settled fact.

title: Nagpur violence after Aurangzeb-tomb protest
summary: Violence broke out in Nagpur after protests by Hindu nationalist groups demanding removal of Aurangzeb's tomb. Associated Press reported injuries to at least 34 police personnel and five other people, damage to homes and vehicles, arrests, and an indefinite curfew in affected areas.
outcome: Police and courts continued processing arrests while the curfew was relaxed. A later report said one person who suffered injuries died in hospital; the record distinguishes that later reported death from the initial riot toll and does not assign collective criminal responsibility to every protest participant.

title: Sabarmati-Agra Superfast derailment near Ajmer
summary: Four coaches and the locomotive of the Sabarmati-Agra Superfast derailed near Madar in Rajasthan's Ajmer district after the train reportedly struck a stationary goods train. No loss of life was reported in the cited contemporaneous account.
outcome: Railway officials restored traffic and examined the derailment site. The record is retained as a non-fatal safety incident and does not infer a final cause beyond the reported collision sequence.

title: Tirupati temple stampede during darshan-token distribution
summary: At least six devotees died and about 35 were injured in a stampede near Tirupati after thousands gathered to obtain free tokens for a special darshan at the Sri Venkateswara temple.
outcome: The Andhra Pradesh government ordered an inquiry. Reporting raised questions about token-counter arrangements and crowd control, but this record does not treat those questions as a final finding of criminal or administrative liability.

title: BJP returns to power in Delhi Assembly election
summary: The BJP won the Delhi Assembly election and returned to government in the National Capital Territory after more than two decades. Associated Press reported the BJP winning 47 of 70 seats on the official count cited in its report.
outcome: The result ended the AAP's uninterrupted Delhi government since 2015 and led to the formation of a BJP government under Rekha Gupta. Secondary summaries have reported a different seat total, so the record links the election source rather than presenting the discrepancy as settled without qualification.

title: Waqf (Amendment) Act passed and partially stayed by Supreme Court
summary: Parliament passed the Waqf (Amendment) Bill in April 2025 after contentious debate over changes to the Waqf Act, including eligibility, board composition, and property-administration rules. The President subsequently assented to the legislation.
outcome: The Supreme Court declined to suspend the entire Act but stayed or limited operation of several provisions in September 2025, including the five-year-practice condition and some powers concerning disputed properties. The constitutional challenge remained pending in the cited material.

title: Bengaluru DJ Halli and KG Halli violence and police firing
summary: Violence followed a social-media post attributed to a relative of Congress MLA Akhanda Srinivasa Murthy. Mobs attacked a police station and the MLA's residence; three people were killed in police firing and a fourth later died from injuries, according to contemporaneous reporting.
outcome: The Karnataka government ordered a magisterial inquiry and later used the UAPA against some accused. Reporting also described extensive property damage, injuries, arrests, curfew, and a subsequent inquiry report; allegations about planning and political links remained contested.

title: Vandalism outside Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal's residence
summary: During a protest by members of the BJP's youth wing outside Arvind Kejriwal's residence, demonstrators breached barricades and damaged the gate and CCTV equipment. Delhi Police arrested eight people initially and later filed a chargesheet against 30 persons, according to reporting.
outcome: The Delhi High Court questioned the police response and later granted bail to eight accused, while the chargesheet preserved the prosecution case. The record describes alleged vandalism and procedural failures without treating arrest or charges as convictions.

title: Police enter Jamia Millia Islamia campus during anti-CAA protests
summary: After violence in areas near Jamia Millia Islamia during protests against the Citizenship Amendment Act, Delhi Police entered the university campus. Students reported injuries from lathi charges and tear gas; a parliamentary record cited 69 injured Jamia students, while police said they were responding to surrounding violence.
outcome: The NHRC deputed a fact-finding team over allegations of illegal detention and denial of medical access. Later court proceedings examined requests for FIRs and an independent probe; the record preserves both the student allegations and the police account.

title: Farmers' Republic Day tractor rally violence
summary: A tractor rally permitted on specified routes entered parts of Delhi early and clashes followed at several locations, including the Red Fort. Police used tear gas and lathis; police records later cited 122 arrests and multiple FIRs, while police personnel and protesters were injured.
outcome: The Delhi High Court directed investigators to conclude proceedings in existing FIRs and later bail orders examined whether individual accused had followed approved routes. The record does not generalize the actions of some participants to the entire farmers' movement.

title: West Bengal post-poll violence and court-directed investigation
summary: After the 2021 West Bengal Assembly election results, residents and opposition workers reported killings, assaults, sexual violence, property damage, and displacement. The NHRC examined complaints under a Calcutta High Court order and submitted a report covering alleged human-rights violations.
outcome: The Calcutta High Court ordered CBI investigation into alleged murder, rape, and attempted-rape cases and constituted an SIT for other matters. The state government disputed the NHRC committee's methodology and alleged bias, so the record presents the findings and the rebuttal together.

title: Kawardha communal violence and curfew
summary: Clashes in Kawardha, Chhattisgarh, followed an altercation involving religious flags and escalated after a rally by Hindu organisations. Reporting described injuries, property damage, detentions, curfew, and internet restrictions; a later march by BJP leaders was alleged to have defied curfew.
outcome: Police registered cases against multiple participants, including BJP leaders, while the state government said the matter was under investigation. The record does not treat political allegations about planning or partisan bias in arrests as established findings.

title: Sandeshkhali allegations, protests, and CBI investigation
category: Political violence and crimes against women
summary: Women in Sandeshkhali reported alleged sexual harassment, land grabbing, assault, and intimidation involving local TMC-linked figures. Protests and BJP mobilisation followed, while an attack on Enforcement Directorate officials became a separate criminal investigation.
outcome: The Calcutta High Court ordered a court-monitored CBI probe and directed a complaint channel for residents; the CBI later filed a chargesheet in the ED-attack case. The record also preserves subsequent allegations that some rape complaints were fabricated or politically induced, which remained contested rather than treating either narrative as universally proved.

title: Dharmasthala mass-burial allegations and SIT inquiry
status: SIT report filed; court process pending
summary: A former sanitation worker alleged that he had been forced to bury bodies, including alleged victims of sexual assault and murder, near Dharmasthala between the 1990s and 2014. Karnataka registered a case and formed an SIT in July 2025; investigators examined locations identified by the complainant and recovered skeletal fragments at one reported site.
outcome: In July 2026, reporting said the SIT filed a 7,000-page final report concluding that the complainant's allegations were false. That is the investigating team's conclusion; the record does not convert it into a judicial acquittal or erase the fact that the allegations triggered a year-long official inquiry.

title: Operation Sindoor and India-Pakistan military escalation
category: Terrorism, national security, and interstate conflict
summary: After the 22 April Pahalgam attack, India announced Operation Sindoor and said it had struck alleged terror infrastructure in Pakistan and Pakistan-administered Kashmir. Pakistan reported retaliatory attacks, and the four-day escalation included missile, drone, and artillery exchanges before a military-to-military ceasefire took effect on 10 May.
outcome: The Indian Ministry of External Affairs confirmed that both sides agreed to stop firing and military action from 1700 IST on 10 May 2025. Claims about targets, damage, and casualties remained disputed between the two governments and are not treated here as independently settled facts.

title: Bihar Assembly election: NDA victory after electoral-roll dispute
summary: The 2025 Bihar Assembly election produced a BJP-led NDA victory. Election reporting based on ECI results put the alliance at 202 seats in the 243-member assembly, including 89 for the BJP. The contest followed a Special Intensive Revision of electoral rolls that opposition parties challenged as potentially exclusionary.
outcome: The ECI reported 67.13% turnout, its highest figure for a Bihar Assembly election since 1951, while ADR's winners report recorded criminal and financial-affidavit data for elected candidates. The roll exercise generated a court-supervised dispute: the ECI published the final roll, while the Supreme Court directed disclosure of names deleted from the draft list and the reasons for deletion. The election result is established; claims that the revision changed the outcome remain political allegations rather than a judicial finding in the cited sources.

title: NAAC accreditation bribery investigation
status: CBI investigation; allegations not final convictions
summary: The CBI arrested the chairman and members of a National Assessment and Accreditation Council inspection committee over allegations that bribes were sought for a favourable institutional rating. Reporting on the FIR described an alleged cumulative demand of Rs 1.80 crore and a proposed A++ accreditation outcome.
outcome: NAAC subsequently barred seven arrested inspection-panel members from further evaluation visits. The case is recorded as an alleged accreditation-integrity failure under investigation; arrests, FIR allegations, and administrative debarment are not treated as proof of guilt or as a completed conviction.

title: Railway departmental-exam bribery case
status: CBI FIR and arrests; investigation pending
summary: The CBI registered a case and arrested six people, including five railway officials, over allegations that candidates were asked for money in exchange for selection in a Western Railway departmental examination. Searches reportedly recovered cash, gold, electronic devices, and documents.
outcome: The case remained an investigation in the cited reporting. The record does not treat the arrests or alleged recovery as proof of guilt, and it separates this departmental recruitment case from the NEET and SSC exam-integrity records.

title: Karur political-rally crowd crush
summary: A crowd crush at a Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam political rally in Karur, Tamil Nadu, killed at least 40 people and injured dozens. The victims included women and children; reporting described a crowd far larger than the venue's expected capacity and questions about arrival timing, access, water, and crowd management.
outcome: The state ordered an investigation and criminal cases were reported against aides. Later reporting described a CBI inquiry into aspects of the event. Responsibility and the final causal findings remained under investigation in the cited material, so this record does not assign sole blame to the party or administration.

title: Nainital child sexual-assault case and law-and-order response
category: Crimes against children and public accountability
status: POCSO case; bail denied; proceedings pending
summary: A 12-year-old girl in Nainital district was allegedly sexually assaulted by an elderly man. The case was registered under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, POCSO Act, and SC/ST Act after the survivor's caste was recorded; protests and violence followed in Nainital.
outcome: The Uttarakhand High Court directed police to supervise the investigation and maintain public order, while a special POCSO court denied bail to the accused in May 2025. The record describes an active criminal proceeding and does not present the allegation as a final conviction.

title: Anjel Chakma killing and dispute over racial-motive findings
summary: Anjel Chakma, a 24-year-old MBA student from Tripura, was attacked in Dehradun on 9 December 2025 and died from his injuries on 26 December. His family and student groups said the assault followed racial slurs; police said their preliminary inquiry found no racial motive and described a heat-of-the-moment altercation.
outcome: Police formed a Special Investigation Team, added murder provisions after Chakma's death, and later filed a chargesheet against six people according to reporting. The NHRC sought a report from the Uttarakhand government. This record keeps the alleged racial context, the police denial, and the ongoing accountability process separate rather than presenting motive as judicially settled.

title: Manipur unrest after Arambai Tenggol arrests and internet shutdown
category: Communal violence, policing, and internet restrictions
summary: Fresh unrest followed the arrest of five leaders of the Meitei group Arambai Tenggol, including Asem Kanan Singh, in June 2025. Authorities imposed curfews and suspended internet and mobile-data services across parts of the Imphal Valley as protests and clashes were reported.
outcome: The administration later relaxed prohibitory orders and the group called off a planned shutdown. The record treats the arrests, restrictions, and reported unrest as established events while leaving the proportionality of the response and competing political explanations open.

title: Operation Black Forest and Abujhmad Maoist encounter
status: Official operation account; encounter circumstances contested
summary: Security forces reported killing 27 CPI (Maoist) members in an encounter in the Abujhmad area of Chhattisgarh, including general secretary Nambala Keshava Rao. The Union government described the operation as a major counterinsurgency success and part of Operation Black Forest.
outcome: The Home Ministry reported additional arrests and surrenders after the operation and renewed its March 2026 deadline for eliminating Naxalism. The entry preserves the official casualty account and the broader security context; an encounter report is not treated as independent proof that every use of force was lawful, and no separate civilian-abuse finding is asserted here without evidence.

title: Sigachi pharmaceutical-factory explosion and safety investigation
summary: An explosion and fire at Sigachi Industries' pharmaceutical plant in Pashamylaram, Telangana, killed workers and injured others. The death toll later reached 54 according to reporting, while early official counts changed as rescue teams recovered bodies from the collapsed unit.
outcome: Police registered a case and later arrested the company's managing director; the Telangana High Court was told that the investigation was progressing but that no arrests had initially been made. Environmental and industrial-safety reports examined the incident. The record does not assign final legal liability while proceedings remain active.

title: Kishtwar cloudburst and Chositi flash flood
summary: A cloudburst triggered flash floods and landslides in Chositi village on the Machail Mata pilgrimage route in Jammu and Kashmir's Kishtwar district. Authorities and the Associated Press reported dozens killed, hundreds rescued, and many people missing as debris overwhelmed a remote pilgrimage base camp.
outcome: Rescue operations continued with the Army, NDRF, police, and local teams, while the death toll changed as recovery work progressed. The record uses the early AP and official-response figures rather than treating later evolving counts as a single final number, and does not infer a cause beyond the reported cloudburst and flash flooding.

title: Uttarkashi Dharali cloudburst and flash flood
summary: A cloudburst over the Kheer Ganga catchment triggered a flash flood and debris flow through Dharali in Uttarkashi district, Uttarakhand. Homes, hotels, roads, bridges, and an Army camp were damaged; early official and news reports recorded deaths, injuries, and dozens of missing people.
outcome: The Army, NDRF, police, and district teams conducted search-and-rescue operations while telecommunications and road links were restored. Later reporting described 67 people still missing and procedures for death certificates; figures changed during the response, so the record does not present one early toll as final.

title: Powai hostage incident involving 17 children
status: Police rescue; fatal police shooting; accountability questions
summary: Mumbai Police rescued 18 people, including 17 children, from a Powai acting studio after a man identified as Rohit Arya allegedly held them inside. Police entered the building during the incident; Arya was shot and later died.
outcome: Reporting said the children were rescued safely. Follow-up coverage linked Arya's prior dispute with Maharashtra's School Education Department over a state education initiative and recorded the police account of the rescue.
